channel.title: Eduard Gorbunov News
channel.description: Research updates, papers, talks, teaching, and community service from Eduard Gorbunov.

item.title: 2026 conference acceptances
item.description:
Several collaborative papers were accepted at 2026 conferences: one at AISTATS, one at ICLR, five at ICML, two at CPAL, and two at UAI.

  • AISTATS 2026: Differentially Private Clipped-SGD.
  • ICLR 2026: High-Probability Bounds for the Last Iterate of Clipped SGD.
  • ICML 2026: Optimization to Generalization under Heavy-Tailed Data; General Analysis of LMO-based Optimizers; Anchored Generalized Optimistic Method; Batch Size in Stochastic Conditional Gradient Methods; Batch Noise, Adaptivity, and Compression under $(L_0,L_1)$-Smoothness.
  • CPAL 2026: Byzantine-Robust Optimization under $(L_0,L_1)$-Smoothness; Federated Learning Can Find Advantageous Friends.
  • UAI 2026: Byzantine-Robust and Differentially Private Federated Optimization; Who to Trust? Federated Distillation.

item.title: New preprint on differentially private Clipped-SGD
item.description: A new preprint studies high-probability convergence with an arbitrary clipping level. arXiv:2507.23512.

item.title: ICLR and ICML 2025 papers, new preprints, and MBZUAI appointment
item.description: Two papers were accepted to ICLR 2025, one paper was accepted to ICML 2025, several preprints were added, and the appointment as Assistant Professor in the Department of Statistics and Data Science at MBZUAI started on August 1, 2025.

item.title: Four NeurIPS 2024 papers and a new preprint
item.description: Four NeurIPS 2024 papers cover federated random reshuffling, Byzantine robustness with partial participation, scale-invariant AdaGrad, and second-order methods for variational inequalities, together with a new preprint on convex $(L_0,L_1)$-smooth optimization.

item.title: Paper accepted to EMNLP 2024 Findings
item.description: The paper "Low-Resource Machine Translation through the Lens of Personalized Federated Learning" was accepted to EMNLP 2024 Findings.
